In the Field...(cont.)

The Western Region Report

It has been a busy month in the Western Region! Stephanie has been working with PSAPs to finalize their FY26 Financial Planning Tools, complete funding reconsiderations, grant workshop, and much more! She has been on the road quite a bit and has enjoyed all the visits with the Western PSAPs!

The Western Region Report

In the Field...(cont.)

PSAP Assessment Coordinator Report

In the Field...(cont.)

During the month of March, Matt completed several PSAP Assessments in Buncombe, Yancey, Mitchell, Caldwell and Alexander Counties. Matt also attended the Resiliency Workshop Series: The Power of Resilience Course in High Point. Matt continues to work on the CAD-to-CAD Initiative and PSAP eligibility requests.

Call NMAC to schedule hosted call handling equipment moves!

Meet the NMAC Team

Hi there! I’m April, an NMAC Tier 1 Network Technician. I’m sure I’ve spoken with a few of you before! I’ve been with NMAC since 2022, and I love being part of the team. Outside of work, I’m all about cozy vibes. I enjoy crocheting and getting lost in relaxing video games. I also share my life (and paycheck) with my adorable 9-year-old cat, Storm; she’s definitely the boss in my home! Looking forward to connecting with more of you!

TALK TO US

1-855-662-2911 911nmac@nc.gov

NMAC News

The Nerd Herd

Renovations Continue....

In the month of March, the old carpet was removed from the NC 911 Board’s office in Raleigh, and new carpet was installed before the the installation of the new cubicals and furniture for the offices and NMAC space. During this time, the NMAC continued to answer calls from 911 centers to assist with any issues experienced with telephony or telephony data analytics.

“Who you gonna call?”

NMAC

When there’s something weird with your call handling equipment, and it don’t look good, call the NMAC at 1-855-662-2911, or email them at 911nmac@nc.gov. The NMAC is a 24/7 operation, and are your first point of contact for all issues, like phone issues, data analytics issues, or 911 Board offered SIP admin CCPs.

Education Station

Telecommunicators Tell Us Their Stories

Angie Turbeville and NC DIT Strategic Communications embarked on a journey to visit several PSAPs, listening to the inspiring stories of telecommunicators from across the state.

Upcoming Training Opportunites

Education Station

911 Board Resiliency Workshop Series O2X: EAT – SWEAT – THRIVE Workshop

Workshop Date/Time: Wednesday, May 28, 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Who should attend: Telecommunicators, Supervisors, Managers, Training Officers Workshop Location: Samarcand Training Academy, 3600 Samarcand Rd., Jackson Springs, NC, 27281

Workshop Information:

Throughout the learning experience, participants will engage with theoretical and research-based concepts, examples of practical applications, and hands-on practical exercises to address a diverse range of learning styles. The program combines classroom education and hands-on application, enabling participants to develop a systematic approach to optimizing mental and physical health, increasing job effectiveness, and promoting career longevity and retention.

EAT - Fueling and hydration for performance, meal planning, and daily implementation of healthy habits for teams and individuals. SWEAT - Pre-habilitation, injury prevention, pain science, recovery for career longevity, conditioning for performance, foundations of movement and lifting, and programming in practice. THRIVE - Optimizing mental performance under pressure, breathing, yoga, integrated restoration (i-rest) for tactical athlete performance, stress factors, behavioral health, building resilience in high-risk jobs, the impact of sleep on performance, and how to create healthy sleep habits.

The 911 Board is paying for the course registration, which is free to the PSAP. This course is not an eligible Emergency Telephone System Fund expenditure, so per diem, lodging, and travel are not eligible. Course registration closes Wednesday, May 21. The registrant will receive a separate email to confirm registration. Cancellations will be accepted until May 21. For any cancellation after this date or any no-show, the PSAP is subject to reimbursing the Board for a cancellation fee.

A confirmation email with dormitory lodging reservation for Samarcand TrainingAcademy will be sent to any registered attendee within five business days of registration. More information on Samarcand Training Academy can be found on their website (https://www.ncdps.gov/dps-services/samarcand-training-academy (opens in new window))

Contact Your Regional Coordinator for more information

Special Attention: Intrado Viper Users VIPER/Power 911 - Online Administrator Training Course

As part of the new AT&T Amendment contract, Intrado will offer VIPER/Power 911 administrator training in May. This training consists of a four-hour online class, with a maximum of five participants per session.

Course Description: This course will teach the fundamentals for modifying, updating, and maintaining configuration data for your VIPER/Power 911 Call-handling solution.

Who should attend: This training is intended for PSAP personnel who manage the phone system and have not previously participated in administrator training.

Dates and Times: Wednesday, May 21, 8 AM to 12 PM Wednesday, May 21, 1 PM to 5 PM Thursday, May 22, 8 AM to 12 PM Thursday, May 22, 1 PM to 5 PM

Click here to register: https://forms.office.com/g/kr6yNCnjVS

Registration closes on Friday, May 16.

For inquires and questions, please contact Angie.Turbeville@nc.gov

The Finance Team

Talking All Things Money.....

NC 911 Board Budget and Service Charge Rate Approval

The NC 911 Board Members approved the FY2026 911 Board Budget, which includes budgeted amounts for continuing NG911 telephony service, including multiple enhancements such as video capabilities and text translation should the PSAP choose to take advantage. The budget also includes costs associated with the PSAP Managers Conference, grant opportunities for PSAPs, statewide projects beneficial to the PSAPs, and the PSAP distribution.

Approved Use of Funds List Updates

Several updates were made to the Training section of the Approved Use of Funds list, and approved by the NC 911 Board during the March 2025 Board meeting. To see all edits, review the Approved Use Of Funds list on the 911 Board’s website.

Finance Team Reminders

CAD-to-CAD Reimbursement Requests

As a reminder, CAD-to-CAD reimbursement requests are due no later than May 15, 2025.

Grant Abstracts

Grant abstracts were due on April 11, 2025 PSAPs who submitted will receive staff responses by April 25, 2025, acknowledging receipt.

Grant Applications Due

The grant application for the FY2026 cycle is due by May 30, 2025, by 11:59 PM. No grant applications will be accepted after this deadline. Be sure to have your eligibility requests submitted by May 1, 2025 to PSAP eligibility for review for any grant projects.

PSAP Highlights

Answering the Call at 30,000 Feet

As a Telecommunicator, you typically have a servant’s heart, and that doesn’t change just because you are on vacation! Ask Tim Roten, a Telecommunicator with the Wilkes County Sheriffs’ Office!

On February 28, 2025, Tim was sitting at the Raleigh-Durham International Airport, awaiting his flight to Las Vegas, Nevada for his well-deserved vacation. While people-watching at the gate, he noticed a female who didn’t look well. She looked pale and was acting rather lethargic. As luck would have it, she boarded his flight. About 45 minutes into the flight, a Delta flight attendant came across the speaker and asked if there was anyone on board with medical experience. Tim looked around, and realized he had to act, even though he was on vacation. Tim spoke up and was ushered to the back of the plane, where he met the female passenger, he had observed in the airport terminal. He began evaluating the 53-year-old female, who was complaining of chest pain (8 out of 10 on the pain scale) and had extremely high blood pressure. I should mention, not only is Tim an EMD, he is also an EMT-B and, prior to his job with Wilkes County, Tim worked as a radiology tech at Atrium Wake Forest Baptist in the Cardiac Cath Lab, so talk about right place at the right time!

Tim was given a medical jump bag and a headset with a direct line to air traffic control, the Delta pilot, and a physician on the ground with Delta airlines. Tim was given orders from the physician to administer chest pain protocol with baby aspirin, nitroglycerin, and oxygen. After three nitroglycerin tablets, the patient’s blood pressure had dropped some, but was still very high. She reported her chest pain had lowered to a 7 out of 10. At that point, the Delta pilot asked Tim his opinion on whether to continue to Las Vegas or divert the flight and land immediately. Tim advised his major concern was that the patient could have a heart attack or a stroke, so he recommended the fight divert to land as soon as possible. The pilot took his advice and immediately asked for permission to land in Tulsa, OK. Upon arrival at the gate, the patient was immediately assessed by paramedics and then transported to the nearest medical facility. Afterward, the flight continued to Las Vegas and Tim had a wonderful time on his vacation!

Tim stated, “Some people might not have spoken up, but I knew I had a duty to act, and I feel my previous medical experience helped me to care for the patient, and my experience as a Telecommunicator helped me to relay information to the pilot, air traffic control and the physician on the ground!”

Answering the Call at 30,000 Feet(Cont.)

Tim has since learned the patient was later released from the hospital and was able to continue her flight to Las Vegas. He was glad to know his swift actions helped to save the life of a fellow passenger and ensure she had more time here with her family. At both work and vacation, Tim “answered the call” to help someone in need.

Way to go, Tim!

Oh Baby!

Congratulations to Chelsie Lewis, PSAP Manager in Madison County on the birth of her baby girl, Jocelyn Charmaine Ann!

You sent us your amazing Talents, and now we are sharing with the entire NC PSAP Community! Thank you for all you do! The NC 911 Board will vote on winners from each category at the April 911 Board Meeting on April 25, 2025. Please take a moment and enjoy these talent submissions!

WANT TO Share WITH US?

We want to hear from you! If you have a story, a topic, or any other ideas for the NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch, reach out to your Regional Coordinator.

We want the NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch to be informative about all things 911 Board, and to highlight and celebrate the successes of 911 here in North Carolina!
